title: The Field Where Growing Is Prayer

features.name: prayer tower
features.desc-on-touch: stalks reaching upward; each one says something the ground planted and forgot

features.name: house at field's margin
features.desc-on-touch: a small dwelling where the farmer left notes about what grew and what the soil remembered

features.name: harvest archive
features.desc-on-touch: a standing directory of what was planted and what prayer grew in its place

features.name: ritual arch
features.desc-on-touch: the doorway the seasons pass through; each crossing is a form of prayer the field speaks

features.name: root glyph cluster
features.desc-on-touch: marks where prayer entered the soil and began speaking in root-language

inscription: here the field learned that growth and prayer are the same utterance spoken in soil's voice

reverie.title: REVERIE — the orchard at dusk where prayer becomes grain
narr: you walk the rows and she names each plant as if it spoke
the glyphs mark where soil learned prayer from root-language
evening falls and the field remembers what it was planted with
you both know the prayer has changed; it grows in the dark now
by morning, the archive will have forgotten what you both saw here

file.name: Agricultural_Prayer_Cycle_Annotations.txt
file.note: records of devotion treated as crop rotation; soil-memory of what was spoken into the ground

exits.label: back down to the root-scripture beneath
toward the field's own seed source, before prayer was spoken
into the harvest-dark where the prayer has grown into something unrecognizable
